message:
feat(packs): add Kong API Gateway pack

- Add apigateway.kong pack with protection for Kong Gateway operations
  - 10 destructive patterns: deck reset, Admin API DELETE endpoints
    (services, routes, plugins, consumers, upstreams, targets, certificates, SNIs)
  - 13 safe patterns for kong CLI, deck CLI read operations
  - 13 unit tests
- Keywords: kong, deck, 8001

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Choose conservative inline capacities based on typical CLI length. Behavior must be identical; only allocation strategy changes.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.8","title":"Advanced: regex-automata/DFA backend for linear patterns","description":"Investigate a DFA-based engine for linear-safe patterns to reduce overhead further.","notes":"This is optional and only pursued if T2/T3/T6 wins plateau.

Abort if complexity outweighs gains or if binary size impact is unacceptable.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.9","title":"Advanced: deterministic rm parser (regex replacement)","description":"Consider replacing rm regex families with a deterministic parser for flags + paths.","notes":"Goal: reduce regex backtracking risk and simplify reasoning. Parser must exactly implement current allow/block rules (temp-dir allowlist, root/home block, flag ordering, long/short flags).

This is only acceptable if behavior is provably identical against the golden corpus.

Testing/Validation:
- Must pass golden corpus + shared e2e harness; targeted rm parity tests in ksk.9.3.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.966607446-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T17:04:23.112095928-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.968154894-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:32.839918432-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:21:16.941956072-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.9.1","title":"Spec parser semantics vs current regex","description":"Write a precise spec mapping regex behavior to parser rules.","notes":"Added rm parser parity spec to docs/pattern-library-design.md (section 8): flag semantics, option terminator, temp allowlist + traversal guard, critical root/home, multi-path handling, and mapping to existing core.filesystem tests.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.99668849-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:45:14.521950821-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:45:14.521950821-05:00","close_reason":"RM parser spec documented with regex parity rules","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.9.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.997835313-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.9.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:51:18.867908938-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.9.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:51:18.91460702-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","title":"[PACK] storage.minio - MinIO Pack","description":"# MinIO Object Storage Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for MinIO (S3-compatible) operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### mc (MinIO Client)
- `mc rb` - Remove bucket
- `mc rb --force` - Force remove
- Remove with recursive flag
- `mc admin bucket delete`

## Safe Patterns
- `mc ls`
- `mc cat`
- `mc stat`
- `mc cp` (copy)
- `mc diff`
- `mc admin info`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: storage.* (git_safety_guard-skt)
- Keywords: mc (MinIO client)
- Note: S3-compatible, similar patterns to aws s3

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for bucket removal
- [ ] Unit tests for recursive deletion
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/storage_minio.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bucket removal blocked
- [ ] Recursive deletion blocked
- [ ] List/copy operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:40.626641176-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T03:36:06.778265327-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T03:36:06.778265327-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented storage.minio pack with 14 safe patterns and 6 destructive patterns. All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.487828632-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.530312907-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.573780863-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.6171008-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.662252443-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.707883469-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.739543506-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.787195064-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rl8","title":"Enforce coverage thresholds in CI (llvm-cov)","description":"## Objective

Turn coverage reporting from informational to **enforced**, with documented thresholds and failure behavior.

## Scope

- Use `cargo llvm-cov` output already produced in CI.
- Define initial minimum thresholds (global + key modules).
- Fail CI when thresholds are not met.

## Notes

- Thresholds should be set **after** baseline audit (see dependencies).
- Keep thresholds modest at first, with a follow-up plan to ratchet.

## Acceptance criteria

- CI fails when coverage drops below defined thresholds.
- Coverage summary still attached as artifact / job summary.
- Thresholds documented in the repo (doc or CI comments).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-08T23:29:52.284596771-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T01:06:50.464637725-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T01:06:50.464637725-05:00","close_reason":"Enforced coverage thresholds in CI and documented them","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rl8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-xqv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T23:31:56.124680168-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rm2n","title":"[TASK] User experience for pack discovery and feedback","description":"# User Experience for Pack Discovery and Feedback

## Scope
Ensure users can easily discover, configure, and provide feedback on packs.

## Deliverables

### 1. Pack Discovery CLI Commands
Implement new dcg commands:
```bash
# List all available packs
dcg packs list

# Show details about a specific pack
dcg packs info secrets.vault

# Show which packs would match a command
dcg explain 'vault secrets disable foo'

# Show packs enabled in current config
dcg packs enabled
```

### 2. Pack Configuration UX
Make configuration intuitive:
```toml
# .dcg.toml example with clear documentation
[packs]
# Enable all Tier 1 packs (critical security)
tier1 = true

# Selectively enable Tier 2 packs
enable = ["dns.cloudflare", "monitoring.datadog"]

# Disable specific packs if needed  
disable = ["backup.rclone"]  # We use custom backup scripts
```

### 3. Helpful Error Messages
When a command is blocked:
```
⚠️  BLOCKED: vault secrets disable production-secrets

Pack: secrets.vault
Reason: 'vault secrets disable' permanently disables a secrets engine,
        potentially causing data loss for all secrets stored there.

This looks intentional? You can:
  1. Add to allowlist: dcg allow 'vault secrets disable production-secrets'
  2. Run with bypass: DCG_BYPASS=1 vault secrets disable production-secrets
  3. Report false positive: https://github.com/.../issues/new?template=false_positive

Docs: https://dcg.dev/packs/secrets/vault
```

### 4. Feedback Collection
- GitHub issue templates for:
  - False positive reports
  - False negative reports  
  - New pack requests
- Telemetry (opt-in) for pattern match statistics
- User survey mechanism

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pack list/info commands implemented
- [ ] Configuration is well-documented
- [ ] Error messages are helpful and actionable
- [ ] Feedback channels are clear and accessible","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- Unit tests for CLI output shape, sorting, and redaction.
- E2E CLI scenarios (list/detail/configure/feedback) using the shared harness with verbose logs.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:35:00.15029369-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T03:18:43.461092146-05:00","comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rm2n","author":"ubuntu","text":"**Progress by SilentRaven (Opus 4.5):**

## Completed:
1. **GitHub Issue Templates** - Created 4 new templates in `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`:
   - `false_positive.yml` - Report legitimate commands incorrectly blocked
   - `false_negative.yml` - Report dangerous commands not being blocked
   - `new_pack_request.yml` - Request new security packs
   - `config.yml` - Template chooser with docs/discussions links

2. **Error Message Enhancement** - Updated `src/hook.rs` to include a 'False positive?' link in denial messages, making it easy for users to report issues

## Already Implemented (verified):
- `dcg packs list` (with --enabled and --verbose flags)
- `dcg pack <id>` (with --patterns flag for details)
- `dcg explain '<command>'` (full decision trace)
- `dcg test '<command>'` (test against enabled packs)
- Helpful error messages with suggestions system
- Configuration in docs/configuration.md

## Pre-existing issues noted:
- Compilation errors in filesystem.rs, cloudfront.rs, scp.rs (unrelated pack work in progress)","created_at":"2026-01-11T08:25:44Z"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","title":"[PACK] email.sendgrid - SendGrid Pack","description":"# SendGrid Email Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for SendGrid CLI and API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### sendgrid CLI / API
- API key deletion
- Template deletion: `DELETE /v3/templates/{id}`
- Domain authentication removal
- Suppression list operations
- Sender identity deletion
- Teammate removal

### Webhook Operations
- Webhook deletion
- Event webhook disabling

## Safe Patterns
- `GET /v3/templates`
- `GET /v3/api_keys`
- Template listing
- Stats retrieval
- Email sending (POST, not destructive to config)

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: email.* (git_safety_guard-62v)
- Keywords: sendgrid
- Note: API-driven, no official CLI

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for template deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for API key deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for domain removal
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/email_sendgrid.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Template deletion blocked
- [ ] API key deletion blocked
- [ ] Domain removal blocked
- [ ] Sending/query operations allowed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:04.483108048-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:04.483108048-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.083500513-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.127704464-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.171107367-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.216548545-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.258989168-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.303102468-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.309864117-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.356907019-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-s1u","title":"E2E: allowlist layering (project/user/system) + conditions","description":"## Objective

Extend E2E coverage to verify allowlist precedence and conditional rules across layers.

## Scope

- Project allowlist already covered; add user/system layer tests using temp HOME + temp /etc overlay (or injection hooks if added).
- Validate precedence: project > user > system.
- Validate conditions/env gating and expiration handling across layers.

## Logging

- Print the layer that matched (when verbose) and reason in failure output.

## Acceptance criteria

- E2E demonstrates correct allowlist precedence and condition handling.
- Tests are hermetic (no reliance on real user allowlists).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan","title":"Epic: Repo scanning + integrations (pre-commit/CI)","description":"# Epic: Repo scanning + integrations (pre-commit/CI)

## Why this epic exists

The hook protects *interactive* command execution, but teams also need protection against destructive commands that get **committed into repositories**:

- CI workflows (`run:` steps) that include dangerous commands
- Dockerfiles (`RUN`) that include irreversible operations
- shell scripts and Makefiles that do destructive cleanup
- Terraform provisioners that run destructive shell

Catching these in review (pre-commit/CI) prevents incidents and makes `dcg` feel like a serious, team-scale safety layer.

Crucially, this must be done **without creating new false positives** that annoy users at org scale.

## Goals

1. Implement `dcg scan` that extracts **executable command contexts** from files and evaluates them using the **same shared evaluator** as hook/CLI.
2. Provide pre-commit and CI workflows that are "warn-first" and configurable (`--fail-on error|warning|none`).
3. Produce high-quality, actionable output:
   - stable rule IDs (`pack_id:pattern_name`)
   - file:line[:col] locations
   - short reason + optional suggestion
4. Keep performance bounded:
   - scan only changed files by default in CI
   - cap file size and number of findings

## Non-goals (initially)

- Building a full static-analysis engine for arbitrary languages.
- Grepping entire repos by default.

## Safety constraints

- Scanners must be extractor-based, not naive substring grep.
- Scanner output must make it obvious what is **executed** vs **documentation**.
- Default behavior should be conservative (warn-first rollout), with hard-fail only for high-confidence catastrophic rules.

## Success Criteria

- `dcg scan --staged` works locally and reports actionable findings.
- A minimal GitHub Actions workflow can run `dcg scan` on PR diffs and surface results.
- Unit tests cover extractor correctness for all MVP file types.

P3 additional extractors (6) remain open for future enhancement."}
